response:
Nurse Emily organized a creative writing workshop for 24 residents, dividing them into groups of 6. This ensures that each group receives personalized attention and support. With 4 groups in total, Emily plans to allocate 3 writing prompts per group for the session. This means she needs 4 groups multiplied by 3 prompts, resulting in 12 initial writing prompts.

To accommodate the possibility that groups may want to continue writing after finishing the initial prompts, Emily has prepared an additional set of 10 extra prompts. These will be shared equally among all the groups. 

In addition to the prompts, Emily also maintains a well-stocked library of writing resources, which includes a variety of writing styles and themes to inspire the residents. The library serves as a valuable tool to enhance the creative experience. Furthermore, the facility’s library is a quiet space where residents can reflect and explore their thoughts before or after the workshop.

In total, Emily needs to have 12 initial prompts plus 10 extra prompts, making the total number of writing prompts 22. 

The END.
